title: Smoky Korean Mackerel Grill
description: Earthy, light, and showoff Korean dish featuring pepper, smoke, and a pescatarian-friendly glaze.
instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and lightly oil the grates.
In a small bowl, whisk together gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, minced garlic, ginger, black pepper, smoked paprika, and sesame oil to create the glaze.
Pat the mackerel fillets dry with paper towels and season both sides with a pinch of salt and a light dusting of black pepper.
Brush each fillet generously with the glaze, reserving a small amount for glazing during cooking.
Place the fillets on the grill skin side down and cook for 4–5 minutes, then flip and brush again with the remaining glaze.
Continue grilling, basting occasionally, until the flesh flakes easily with a fork (about 5–7 minutes total).
Remove from heat and let rest for 2 minutes.
Transfer to a serving platter, drizzle any remaining glaze, sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds and sliced green onions, and serve immediately with a side of steamed rice or a light salad.
